# B-Trees

## 2-3 Trees
- node can have 2 or 3 children
- keys = children - 1
- 2-node: contains 1 key
	- has 2 children (one for values $< k_1$, one for values $> k_1$)
- 3-node: contains 2 key
	- has 3 children
		- values $< k_1$
		- values between $k_1$ and $k_2$
		- values $> k_1$
## 2-3-4 Trees
- node can have 2, 3, or 4 children
- add the 4-node on top of 2-3 trees

## Insertion
1. If one node has 4 keys, then it needs splitting
	1. Promote: choose one to live with parent ==the one you choose need to split half for rest. 所以习惯选第一个中间数，为了保持左倾习惯，以及算法简单统一
	2. Split:
